title: Remote control authority and authentication
abstract: The invention relates to systems and methods to enable a mobile device to be used as a remote control, in order to control one or more remotely controllable objects. In possible embodiments of the invention, proximity-based (or other) remote controls include control authorization to enable the transfer or sharing of control between different remote controls being implemented on different mobile devices, in order to permit different users to transfer or share control of a same remotely controllable object, for example. In other possible embodiments of the invention, a proximity-based (or other) remote control uses control authentication to enable the pairing of a remote control implemented on a device and a remotely controllable object, for example. Various embodiments of control authority and control authentication are possible and anticipated by the invention to address a wide range of practical remote control applications and provide many benefits to users.
claims: 1. A method performed by at least one computer processor executing computer program instructions stored on at least one non-transitory computer-readable medium to perform a method, the method comprising: (1) at a first time, determining that a first mobile device is in proximity to a first remotely controllable object; (2) in response to determining that the first mobile device is in proximity to the first object, at the first mobile device, presenting a first remote control user interface relevant to the first object that enables a user to remotely control the first remotely controllable object; (3) receiving a first input from the user via a first remote control user interface element of the first remote control user interface, the first input indicating a first action to be performed at the first object; (4) in response to receiving the first input, wirelessly transmitting, to a first remote server, a first communication relating to the first action; (5) at a second time after the first time, determining that proximity does not exist between the first mobile device and the first remotely controllable object; (6) at the first mobile device: (6) (a) in response to determining that proximity does not exist, disabling at least the first remote control user interface element of the first remote control user interface for controlling the first remotely controllable object, to thereby terminate control of the first remotely controllable object by means of the first remote control user interface presented at the first mobile device; (6) (b) presenting a visual indication indicating that the first remote control user interface element of a first remote control user interface for controlling the first remotely controllable object is disabled.
